---
title: "prelim_analysis"
authors: Evan Ferris, Laure Etienne
format: html
editor: visual
---

## Quarto

Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished document. To learn more about Quarto see <https://quarto.org>.

## Setup

We load the tidyverse for data manipulation and visualization, and lubridate for handling dates.

```{r setup}
library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)
library(readr)
```

## Data Import

We load the three tables from the Portal Project Teaching Database: survey observations, plot metadata, and species information.

```{r load-data}
surveys <- read_csv("surveys.csv")
plots   <- read_csv("plots.csv")
species <- read_csv("species.csv")

glimpse(surveys)
glimpse(plots)
glimpse(species)
```

## Data Tidying

The surveys table contains missing values in \`species_id\`, \`sex\`, \`hindfoot_length\`, and \`weight\`. We remove rows where \`species_id\` is missing since these cannot be joined to the species table, and flag missing values in the numeric columns. We also combine the separate \`year\`, \`month\`, and \`day\` columns into a single date column, and convert \`sex\` and \`species_id\` to factors.

```{r tidy-data}
surveys_clean <- surveys |>
  filter(!is.na(species_id)) |>
  mutate(date = make_date(year, month, day)) |>
  mutate(
    sex        = factor(sex, levels = c("M", "F")),
    species_id = factor(species_id)
  )

surveys_clean |>
  summarise(
    missing_sex             = sum(is.na(sex)),
    missing_hindfoot_length = sum(is.na(hindfoot_length)),
    missing_weight          = sum(is.na(weight))
  )
```

## Joins

We join the cleaned surveys table to the species table using \`species_id\` as the shared key. This adds genus, species name, and taxa information to each observation. We then join the plots table using \`plot_id\` to add plot treatment type.

```{r join-data}
surveys_joined <- surveys_clean |>
  left_join(species, by = "species_id") |>
  left_join(plots, by = "plot_id")

glimpse(surveys_joined)
```

## Data Visualization

We first plot the number of observations recorded each year to get asense of how sampling effort changed over time.

```{r observations-over-time}

```

We next examine how observations are distributed across different taxonomic groups to understand the dataset.

```{r observations-by-taxa}

```

The dataset is dominated by rodent observations, which shows the ecological focus of the Portal Project study site. We filter to rodents only for the remainder of the analysis.

```{r filter-rodents}

```

We remove rows with missing values in the key analysis columns: weight, hindfoot_length, and sex. These rows cannot contribute meaningfully to our analysis across species and plot treatments.

```{r handle-missing}

```

We now redo the graphs from earlier using the newly-cleaned rodent data, though we will not use the observations by taxa graph in the final analysis because we are only going to view rodents anyways.

```{r observations-over-time-rodents}

```

```{r observations-by-taxa-rodents}

```
